content.problemStatement:
After a long time, Chef has finally decided to renovate his house. Chef's house has **N** rooms in it numbered from 1 to **N**. Each room is currently painted in one of the red, blue or green colors. Your are given configuration of colors of his house by a string **S** consisting of **N** characters. In this string, color red will be denoted by 'R', green by 'G' and blue by 'B'.



Chef does not like current painting configuration that much and would like to repaint the house such that each room has same color. 

For painting, Chef has all the 3 color paints available and mixing any 2 color paints will result into 3rd color paint i.e


R + B = G
B + G = R
G + R = B
 

For example, painting a room having red color before with green color paint will make the color of room blue.



Also, Chef has many buckets of paint of each color. Simply put, you can assume that he will not run out of paint.


Being extraordinary lazy, our little chef does not want to work much and therefore, he has asked you to find the minimum number of rooms he has to repaint **(possibly zero)** in order to have all the rooms with same color. Can you please help him?
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N (the number of rooms). The second line contains a string S of length N consisting only of characters 'R', 'G' and 'B' representing the current colors of the rooms.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single integer on a new line – the minimum number of rooms that need to be repainted so that all rooms end up with the same color.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test 1: repaint room 2 with blue paint (R + B = G) to make all rooms red. Minimum 1 repaint. Test 2: all rooms already red, 0 repaint. Test 3: repaint room 1 with green paint (R + G = B) and room 2 with red paint (G + R = B) to make all rooms blue, total 2 repaint.

solution.approach: For each test case count how many rooms already have each of the three colors. If we decide to make all rooms color X, we only need to repaint the rooms that are not X. The optimal choice is the color with the highest current frequency because it minimises the number of repaint operations. Hence answer = N - max(cntR, cntG, cntB). This is O(N) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, total O(∑N) over all test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space besides the input string.

code.Python3:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().split()
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it))
        s = next(it)
        cntR = s.count('R')
        cntG = s.count('G')
        cntB = s.count('B')
        mx = max(cntR, cntG, cntB)
        out_lines.append(str(N - mx))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
